title: AI That Actually Helps You Study

modules.title: What AI Can and Can't Do for You
modules.sub: Set realistic expectations before you start using AI
modules.description: Learners map out where AI genuinely adds value in academic work versus where it falls short or creates risk. You leave with a honest mental model that stops you from over-relying on tools that can confidently give you wrong answers.

modules.title: Using AI to Actually Understand Lectures
modules.sub: Turn confusing notes and recordings into real comprehension
modules.description: You practice feeding lecture notes, transcripts, and slides into AI tools to generate plain-language summaries, clarify jargon, and surface the concepts you actually missed. By the end you have a repeatable after-lecture routine that replaces passive rereading.

modules.title: Research Without the Rabbit Holes
modules.sub: Find credible sources faster without losing hours online
modules.description: Learners use AI to break down a research question, identify search angles, and evaluate whether a source is worth reading before committing to it. You build a workflow that gets you from assignment prompt to a solid source list without the usual three-hour detour.

modules.title: Writing With AI Without Getting Caught
modules.sub: Use AI as a writing coach, not a ghostwriter
modules.description: You explore exactly where the academic integrity line sits and practice using AI for brainstorming, outlining, and feedback rather than generation. The module gives you concrete techniques that strengthen your own writing voice while keeping you clearly on the right side of your institution's policies.

modules.title: Studying Smarter With AI Quizzes
modules.sub: Generate personalized practice questions from your own material
modules.description: Learners prompt AI tools to create flashcards, practice tests, and Socratic-style challenges directly from their notes and readings. You test different prompting strategies and identify which formats actually stick for your learning style.

modules.title: Managing Deadlines and Overwhelm
modules.sub: Use AI to plan workloads before stress takes over
modules.description: You use AI to break a packed week into prioritized tasks, spot conflicts before they become crises, and draft realistic study schedules around real constraints. The module also addresses what to do when the plan falls apart, so one bad day doesn't spiral.

modules.title: Group Projects Made Less Painful
modules.sub: Coordinate teams and clarify roles with AI support
modules.description: Learners apply AI tools to common group-project bottlenecks: drafting shared outlines, summarizing everyone's contributions, and preparing talking points for team check-ins. You walk away with templates you can drop into any collaborative assignment immediately.

modules.title: Build Your Own AI Study Stack
modules.sub: Assemble a personal toolkit that fits your courses and style
modules.description: You audit the tools and techniques from the whole course and select the combination that matches your major, workload, and learning preferences. The final deliverable is a documented personal AI study system you can refine every semester.

synopsis: College is expensive, exhausting, and competitive â AI won't do the work for you, but it can make the work you do dramatically more effective. This course shows you the specific tools and techniques that actually help with real coursework, not hypothetical scenarios.
rationale: College students are adopting AI tools rapidly but without guidance on what's genuinely useful, what risks academic integrity, and how to build habits that transfer into their careers.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can set up a personalized AI-assisted study system that helps you learn faster, write stronger papers, and manage your workload without crossing academic integrity lines.
